title: Polystichum × subalpinum
intro: Polystichum × subalpinum, also known as the hybrid autumn fern, is a sterile hybrid of the autumn fern (Polystichum aculeatum) and the mountain spleenwort fern (Polystichum seiferum). The health benefits of Polystichum × subalpinum include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for bronchitis and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other polyphenolic compounds that contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Polystichum × subalpinum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Polystichum × subalpinum is a nothospecies of the fern family, Dryopteridaceae. It is a hybrid of Polystichum lonchitis and Polystichum setiferum. The classification of Polystichum × subalpinum as a nothospecies indicates its unique and hybrid genetic makeup. The hybrid status of Polystichum × subalpinum is often used in horticulture to create desirable traits in cultivated ferns. As a result, Polystichum × subalpinum is of interest to botanists and horticulturists studying fern biology and plant breeding.
